title: Spanish Peppers Paella
description: A deliciously juicy and floral Spanish dish featuring peppers, with an al-dente finish, perfect for vegetarians. This Andalusian-inspired paella is energizing and full of flavor.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped Spanish peppers and cook until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped tomato, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the soaked saffron threads and their liquid, and stir to combine.
Add the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Add the uncooked Arborio rice and stir to combine.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the rice is al-dente and the liquid has been absorbed.
